title: Santoshi Chalisa
intro: Shri Santoshi Chalisa - Hymn to Goddess Santoshi
description:
॥ Shri Santoshi Mata Chalisa (English Transliteration) ॥

Doha
Bandau Santoshi charan Riddhi-Siddhi dataar.
Dhyan dharat hi hot nar dukh saagar se paar.
Bhaktan ko santosh de Santoshi tav naam.
Kripa karhu Jagadamb ab aaya tere dhaam.

Chaupai
Jai Santoshi maat anupam.
Shanti dayini roop manoram.
Sundar varan chaturbhuj roopa.
Vesh manohar lalit anupa.
Shwetambar roop manhari.
Maa tumhari chhavi jag se nyari.
Divya swaroopa aayat lochan.
Darshan se ho sankat mochan.
Jai Ganesh ki suta Bhavani.
Riddhi-Siddhi ki putri gyani.
Agam agochar tumhari maya.
Sab par karo kripa ki chhaya.
Naam anek tumhare mata.
Akhil vishwa hai tumko dhyata.
Tumne roop anekon dhare.
Ko kahi sake charitra tumhare.
Dham anek kahan tak kahiye.
Sumiran tab karke sukh lahiye.
Vindhyachal mein Vindhyavasini.
Koteshwar Saraswati suhasini.
Kalkatte mein tu hi Kali.
Dusht nashini mahakarali.
Sambhal pur Bahuchara kahati.
Bhaktjanon ka dukh mitati.
Jwala ji mein Jwala Devi.
Pujat nitya bhakt jan sevi.
Nagar Bambai ki maharani.
Maha Lakshmi tum kalyani.
Madura mein Meenakshi tum ho.
Sukh dukh sabki sakshi tum ho.
Rajnagar mein tum Jagadambe.
Bani Bhadrakali tum Ambe.
Pavagarh mein Durga mata.
Akhil vishwa tera yash gaata.
Kashi Puradhishwari mata.
Annapurna naam suhata.
Sarvanand karo kalyani.
Tumhi Sharada amrit vani.
Tumhari mahima jal mein thal mein.
Dukh daridra sab meto pal mein.
Jete rishi aur munisha.
Narad Dev aur Devesha.
Is jagati ke nar aur nari.
Dhyan dharat hain maat tumhari.
Japar kripa tumhari hoti.
Vah paata bhakti ka moti.
Dukh daridra sankat mit jaata.
Dhyan tumhara jo jan dhyata.
Jo jan tumhari mahima gaave.
Dhyan tumhara kar sukh paave.
Jo man rakhe shuddh bhavana.
Taki puran karo kaamna.
Kumati nivari sumati ki daatri.
Jayati jayati mata Jagdhatri.
Shukravar ka divas suhavan.
Jo vrat kare tumhara pavan.
Gud chhole ka bhog lagave.
Katha tumhari sune sunave.
Vidhavat puja kare tumhari.
Phir prasad pave shubhkari.
Shakti-samarth ho jo dhanko.
Daan-dakshina de vipran ko.
Ve jagati ke nar au nari.
Manvanchit phal pave bhari.
Jo jan sharan tumhari jave.
So nishchay bhav se tar jave.
Tumharo dhyan kumari dhyave.
Nishchay manvanchit var pave.
Sadhava puja kare tumhari.
Amar suhagin ho vah nari.
Vidhava dhar ke dhyan tumhara.
Bhavsagar se utare para.
Jayati jayati jay sankat harni.
Vighna vinashan mangal karni.
Hum par sankat hai ati bhari.
Vegi khabar lo maat hamari.
Nishdin dhyan tumharo dhyata.
Deh bhakti var hum ko mata.
Yeh Chalisa jo nit gaave.
So bhavsagar se tar jave.
meaning:
॥ Shri Santoshi Mata Chalisa (English Transliteration & Meaning) ॥

Doha
Bandau Santoshi charan Riddhi-Siddhi dataar.
(I worship the feet of Santoshi, the giver of Riddhi (prosperity) and Siddhi (success).)
Dhyan dharat hi hot nar dukh saagar se paar.
(By merely meditating on Her, man crosses the ocean of sorrows.)
Bhaktan ko santosh de Santoshi tav naam.
(She who gives satisfaction to devotees is named Santoshi.)
Kripa karhu Jagadamb ab aaya tere dhaam.
(O Mother of the World, bestow your grace, now that I have come to your abode.)

Chaupai
Jai Santoshi maat anupam.
(Victory to the incomparable Mother Santoshi.)
Shanti dayini roop manoram.
(Your form is the giver of peace and is very beautiful.)
Sundar varan chaturbhuj roopa.
(You have a beautiful complexion and a four-armed form.)
Vesh manohar lalit anupa.
(Your attire is charming, graceful, and matchless.)
Shwetambar roop manhari.
(In white garments, your form steals the mind.)
Maa tumhari chhavi jag se nyari.
(O Mother, your radiance is unique in the world.)
Divya swaroopa aayat lochan.
(You are divine in form with large, beautiful eyes.)
Darshan se ho sankat mochan.
(By your vision, all troubles are resolved.)
Jai Ganesh ki suta Bhavani.
(Victory to you, O Bhavani, daughter of Ganesha.)
Riddhi-Siddhi ki putri gyani.
(You are the wise daughter of Riddhi and Siddhi.)
Agam agochar tumhari maya.
(Your illusion (Maya) is unfathomable and beyond reach.)
Sab par karo kripa ki chhaya.
(Cast the shadow of your grace upon everyone.)
Naam anek tumhare mata.
(O Mother, you have many names.)
Akhil vishwa hai tumko dhyata.
(The entire universe meditates upon you.)
Tumne roop anekon dhare.
(You have assumed many forms.)
Ko kahi sake charitra tumhare.
(Who can describe your character/deeds?)
Dham anek kahan tak kahiye.
(How far should I speak of your many abodes?)
Sumiran tab karke sukh lahiye.
(One gains happiness only by remembering you.)
Vindhyachal mein Vindhyavasini.
(In Vindhyachal, you are Vindhyavasini.)
Koteshwar Saraswati suhasini.
(In Koteshwar, you reside as the smiling Saraswati.)
Kalkatte mein tu hi Kali.
(In Kolkata, you are Kali.)
Dusht nashini mahakarali.
(You are the destroyer of the wicked, the great Mahakarali.)
Sambhal pur Bahuchara kahati.
(In Sambhalpur, you are called Bahuchara.)
Bhaktjanon ka dukh mitati.
(You erase the sorrows of your devotees.)
Jwala ji mein Jwala Devi.
(In Jwalaji, you are Jwala Devi.)
Pujat nitya bhakt jan sevi.
(Devotees worship you daily with service.)
Nagar Bambai ki maharani.
(You are the Queen of the city of Mumbai.)
Maha Lakshmi tum kalyani.
(O auspicious one, you are Maha Lakshmi.)
Madura mein Meenakshi tum ho.
(In Madurai, you are Meenakshi.)
Sukh dukh sabki sakshi tum ho.
(You are the witness of everyone's joys and sorrows.)
Rajnagar mein tum Jagadambe.
(In Rajnagar, you are Jagadambe.)
Bani Bhadrakali tum Ambe.
(O Mother, you became Bhadrakali.)
Pavagarh mein Durga mata.
(In Pavagarh, you are Mother Durga.)
Akhil vishwa tera yash gaata.
(The entire world sings your glory.)
Kashi Puradhishwari mata.
(In Kashi, you are the Mother, the Ruler of the City.)
Annapurna naam suhata.
(Your name Annapurna is very pleasing.)
Sarvanand karo kalyani.
(O auspicious one, you provide joy to all.)
Tumhi Sharada amrit vani.
(You are Sharada, the voice of nectar.)
Tumhari mahima jal mein thal mein.
(Your glory is in water and on land.)
Dukh daridra sab meto pal mein.
(You erase all sorrows and poverty in an instant.)
Jete rishi aur munisha.
(All the sages and wise men.)
Narad Dev aur Devesha.
(Narada, gods, and the Lord of gods meditate upon you.)
Is jagati ke nar aur nari.
(The men and women of this world.)
Dhyan dharat hain maat tumhari.
(Meditate upon you, O Mother.)
Japar kripa tumhari hoti.
(He upon whom your grace falls.)
Vah paata bhakti ka moti.
(He finds the pearl of devotion.)
Dukh daridra sankat mit jaata.
(Sorrow, poverty, and crises vanish.)
Dhyan tumhara jo jan dhyata.
(For the person who meditates on you.)
Jo jan tumhari mahima gaave.
(The person who sings your glory.)
Dhyan tumhara kar sukh paave.
(Attains happiness by meditating on you.)
Jo man rakhe shuddh bhavana.
(One who keeps a pure intention in their heart.)
Taki puran karo kaamna.
(You fulfill their wishes.)
Kumati nivari sumati ki daatri.
(Remover of bad thoughts, giver of good wisdom.)
Jayati jayati mata Jagdhatri.
(Victory, victory to you, O Mother, Supporter of the World.)
Shukravar ka divas suhavan.
(Friday is a pleasant day.)
Jo vrat kare tumhara pavan.
(One who observes your holy fast.)
Gud chhole ka bhog lagave.
(Offers the food of jaggery and chickpeas.)
Katha tumhari sune sunave.
(Listens to and narrates your story.)
Vidhavat puja kare tumhari.
(Worships you according to the proper rituals.)
Phir prasad pave shubhkari.
(Then receives the auspicious prasad.)
Shakti-samarth ho jo dhanko.
(According to one's wealth and capacity.)
Daan-dakshina de vipran ko.
(Gives donations and alms to the Brahmins.)
Ve jagati ke nar au nari.
(Those men and women of this world.)
Manvanchit phal pave bhari.
(Receive the desired fruits abundantly.)
Jo jan sharan tumhari jave.
(Whoever goes under your shelter.)
So nishchay bhav se tar jave.
(They certainly cross over this worldly existence.)
Tumharo dhyan kumari dhyave.
(The young maiden who meditates on you.)
Nishchay manvanchit var pave.
(Certainly finds the groom of her choice.)
Sadhava puja kare tumhari.
(The married woman who worships you.)
Amar suhagin ho vah nari.
(That woman remains an eternally happy wife.)
Vidhava dhar ke dhyan tumhara.
(The widow who meditates on you.)
Bhavsagar se utare para.
(Crosses over the ocean of life.)
Jayati jayati jay sankat harni.
(Victory, victory, O remover of crises.)
Vighna vinashan mangal karni.
(Destroyer of obstacles, doer of auspicious deeds.)
Hum par sankat hai ati bhari.
(We are in a very great crisis.)
Vegi khabar lo maat hamari.
(O Mother, please take care of us soon.)
Nishdin dhyan tumharo dhyata.
(I meditate on you day and night.)
Deh bhakti var hum ko mata.
(Grant me the boon of devotion, O Mother.)
Yeh Chalisa jo nit gaave.
(Whoever sings this Chalisa daily.)
So bhavsagar se tar jave.
(Crosses over the ocean of life.)

faq.q1: Why is the Santoshi Chalisa read?
faq.a1: It is primarily read to invite family happiness, mutual love, and a sense of contentment into one's life.
faq.q2: Is the Friday fast mandatory to read the Chalisa?
faq.a2: No, fasting is not mandatory to read the Chalisa. You can read it with devotion at any time, although reciting it on Fridays is considered especially rewarding.
faq.q3: Does reading it help resolve financial or family problems?
faq.a3: Yes, by the grace of Maa Santoshi, family conflicts are reduced, and it brings both financial and mental peace into your life.
faq.q4: What is the best time to read it?
faq.a4: Friday is the best day for this. You can recite it on Friday morning or evening with a calm mind.
faq.a5: Yes, it is excellent for children to read as it helps develop patience, contentment, and good moral values in them.
